messages.author: mibzzer15
messages.text: Got my esp32-c6 device today. I have flashed the firmware and confirmed I can access the UI. I am out of time this weekend but want to ask a few questions before I go to test it this week or next weekend. For the updating process - You upload the zip file to the esp through the wifi UI. I THINK (please confirm) the right process on the repeater is to go ahead and put it into DFU mode by sending the "start ota" command on the command line. I then fly the drone with the ESP32 device attached out to the repeater. Stay near enough for it to connect, transfer files and finish the update? If that is all correct, how do I know its done? Just wait with the drone for a few minutes and let things happen? Check the logs after to confirm?
messages.content_html: Got my esp32-c6 device today. I have flashed the firmware and confirmed I can access the UI. I am out of time this weekend but want to ask a few questions before I go to test it this week or next weekend. For the updating process - You upload the zip file to the esp through the wifi UI. I THINK (please confirm) the right process on the repeater is to go ahead and put it into DFU mode by sending the "start ota" command on the command line. I then fly the drone with the ESP32 device attached out to the repeater. Stay near enough for it to connect, transfer files and finish the update? If that is all correct, how do I know its done? Just wait with the drone for a few minutes and let things happen? Check the logs after to confirm?
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: cra0
messages.text: hey @mibzzer15 thanks for the questions. Yes I will write up a proper guide for this but this will have to do for now since I'm short on time: 1. When the esp32 starts up for the first time it checks if it has a valid package, if not it starts in AP (Access Point) mode where there is a WI-FI access point for you to connect to (for now called drone-updater with meshcore123 as the password). 2. You connect to that AP and go to the url http://192.168.4.1/ui and upload your zip package for the target meshcore node you want to flash. 3. You then can press reboot to bluetooth or powercycle the esp32 it will then start its scanning constantly. You can pause the scan at any time by pressing the program/boot button. Holding down that button for 5 seconds will force it back into AP mode. 4. You then yes login to the repeaters admin panel and go into the console and write start ota it will then give you the MAC address of the bluetooth advert. 5. You then fly the drone or bring the device close to the repeater (if you have an external antenna it can work up to 10 meters) 6. Typically the update takes 10 seconds but you can wait as long as you like. Right now there is no way to tell what happened this is a problem for later for me to solve 7. If you then try to login again to the device on the meshcore app and it worked and the clock was reset you can tell it worked. 8. To confirm it worked you type ver in the console 9. Then when you get the drone back you can always put it back into AP mode by holding the program/boot button for 5secs then view the logs to see what happened. I hope this helps
messages.content_html: hey @mibzzer15 thanks for the questions. Yes I will write up a proper guide for this but this will have to do for now since I'm short on time: 1. When the esp32 starts up for the first time it checks if it has a valid package, if not it starts in AP (Access Point) mode where there is a WI-FI access point for you to connect to (for now called drone-updater with meshcore123 as the password). 2. You connect to that AP and go to the url http://192.168.4.1/ui and upload your zip package for the target meshcore node you want to flash. 3. You then can press reboot to bluetooth or powercycle the esp32 it will then start its scanning constantly. You can pause the scan at any time by pressing the program/boot button. Holding down that button for 5 seconds will force it back into AP mode. 4. You then yes login to the repeaters admin panel and go into the console and write start ota it will then give you the MAC address of the bluetooth advert. 5. You then fly the drone or bring the device close to the repeater (if you have an external antenna it can work up to 10 meters) 6. Typically the update takes 10 seconds but you can wait as long as you like. Right now there is no way to tell what happened this is a problem for later for me to solve 7. If you then try to login again to the device on the meshcore app and it worked and the clock was reset you can tell it worked. 8. To confirm it worked you type ver in the console 9. Then when you get the drone back you can always put it back into AP mode by holding the program/boot button for 5secs then view the logs to see what happened. I hope this helps

messages.author: mibzzer15
messages.text: I don't know much about small electronics like this (just enough to get in trouble) but maybe since this will be mounted to a drone - can you setup 2 status LEDs that could be installed in such a way that is visible to the camera on the drone? 1 LED would be the board mode/status light and the other LED would be file transfer status.
messages.content_html: I don't know much about small electronics like this (just enough to get in trouble) but maybe since this will be mounted to a drone - can you setup 2 status LEDs that could be installed in such a way that is visible to the camera on the drone? 1 LED would be the board mode/status light and the other LED would be file transfer status.

messages.author: cra0
messages.text: its definitely do able and currently the esp32-c6 for example has a blinking indicator to see what is going on but that was more so for debugging. the other idea is i could set one of the GPIO pins to be a status indicator and you can wire a LED with a small resister and position it close to the camera of the drone i guess that can tell you what is going on. But i wanted to keep the initial release very basic and to the point.
messages.content_html: its definitely do able and currently the esp32-c6 for example has a blinking indicator to see what is going on but that was more so for debugging. the other idea is i could set one of the GPIO pins to be a status indicator and you can wire a LED with a small resister and position it close to the camera of the drone i guess that can tell you what is going on. But i wanted to keep the initial release very basic and to the point.

messages.author: cra0
messages.text: all I noticed from the earlier logs is the DFU write is slow on some bootloaders like the ADAFruit one you had before you flashed OTAFix. I updated to add a delay before DFU write happens from the esp32 side of things. will push a new release which should help some edgecases.
messages.content_html: all I noticed from the earlier logs is the DFU write is slow on some bootloaders like the ADAFruit one you had before you flashed OTAFix. I updated to add a delay before DFU write happens from the esp32 side of things. will push a new release which should help some edgecases.

messages.content_html: Ready for its maiden voyage tomorrow.

messages.author: mibzzer15
messages.text: It works! Amazing! This is awesome!
messages.content_html: It works! Amazing! This is awesome!

messages.author: cra0
messages.text: Haha awesome work. Do you mind if I save some photos along with mine for a docs page?
messages.content_html: Haha awesome work. Do you mind if I save some photos along with mine for a docs page?

messages.author: mibzzer15
messages.text: Yeah go ahead!
messages.content_html: Yeah go ahead!

messages.author: mibzzer15
messages.text: Just wanted to say thank you for developing this! I just finished updating my last of 7 repeaters to 1.14.1 to get everything on 2-byte mode in the SF Bay area. Thank you again! (edited)
messages.content_html: Just wanted to say thank you for developing this! I just finished updating my last of 7 repeaters to 1.14.1 to get everything on 2-byte mode in the SF Bay area. Thank you again! (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: cra0
messages.text: Your message means a lot no worries im glad I could help. It brings me confidence now to deploy nodes in high to reach places without worrying about them not being accessible for updates.
messages.content_html: Your message means a lot no worries im glad I could help. It brings me confidence now to deploy nodes in high to reach places without worrying about them not being accessible for updates.

messages.author: mibzzer15
messages.text: Just drop it the OTAfix bootloader, throw it up in the hard to reach area and you'll be good to go. This system works great! We will have to work on something that will help report status of the flashing when its in progress but otherwise it works very well right now!
messages.content_html: Just drop it the OTAfix bootloader, throw it up in the hard to reach area and you'll be good to go. This system works great! We will have to work on something that will help report status of the flashing when its in progress but otherwise it works very well right now!

messages.author: cra0
messages.text: Yeah about that a short term solution is the device spawns an access point you can connect to and see progress but in terms of distance im not sure how far that will work
messages.content_html: Yeah about that a short term solution is the device spawns an access point you can connect to and see progress but in terms of distance im not sure how far that will work

embeds_html:
Release Release Beta 0.9 · cra0/esp32-nordic-ota-dfu
Release Beta 0.9 This release hardens the post-DFU activate path against the OTAFIX bootloader settings-page race that can leave a target stuck advertising as *_DFU after a successful 100% transfer...
